name: separated
description: Jacob Soboroff's deeply reported account of the Trump administration's family separation policy — from the policy's creation in Washington to the cages at the border, the traumatized children, and the failed reunification. Based on the author's firsthand reporting that helped end the policy.
useCase: Understanding the family separation policy, the human cost of zero tolerance immigration enforcement, the conditions in detention facilities, and the legal battle for reunification.
knownRisks: Graphic descriptions of child trauma may be disturbing
The author was an MSNBC reporter covering the story — this is a journalistic account, not an academic history
Some details rely on the memories of sources with their own motivations
Strongly critical of the Trump administration — not a neutral account
Names of some individuals have been changed to protect their safety
